metaTitle: Boiler Not Igniting | Common Causes and Fixes
metaDescription: Boiler not igniting? Find out what the fault may mean, what you can check safely and when to call an engineer.
h1: Why Is My Boiler Not Igniting?
intro: If your boiler is not igniting, the appliance is failing to light and start the heating cycle properly. The cause may be simple, but repeated failure to ignite needs proper diagnosis.
safetyWarning: Do not keep forcing resets if the boiler repeatedly fails to ignite.
symptoms: Boiler tries to start but does not fire
No heating and no hot water
safeChecks: Check whether there is power to the boiler and controls
Check the room thermostat or programmer settings
Look for obvious low-pressure warnings on the display
doNotDiy: Do not interfere with ignition parts or gas components
Do not dismantle the boiler casing
Do not keep resetting the boiler over and over
typicalFixes: Restoring correct pressure and controls operation
Resolving component or electrical issues affecting start-up

sections.heading: What ignition failure means
sections.body: Ignition failure means the boiler is not completing the normal firing sequence. That can be caused by pressure issues, electrical faults, failed components or brand-specific lockout behaviour.

sections.heading: Why the boiler may stop firing
sections.body: Low system pressure, faulty ignition parts, sensor issues and controls faults are all common reasons a boiler will not light when called for heat.

sections.heading: When this becomes urgent
sections.body: If you have no heating or hot water and the boiler repeatedly locks out, book a repair visit rather than continuing to reset it and hope it recovers.
